news.summary: A report titled "Wiring Devices Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market for Wiring Devices is projected to grow from US$ 58.36 billion in 2024 to US$ 100.03 billion by 2033, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.17% from 2025 to 2033. This growth is attributed to factors such as urbanization, infrastructure development, increasing adoption of smart home technology, rising demand for energy-efficient solutions, government regulations promoting energy conservation, and increasing industrial activities.
news.headline: Wiring devices market to hit $100B by 2033, growing at 6.17% CAGR from 2025. Smart homes, IoT, energy efficiency drive growth.

news.summary: The global Artificial Intelligence market is anticipated to experience substantial growth, with projections to reach $2.53 trillion by 2033, up from $184.15 billion in 2024. This growth is forecasted at a compound annual growth rate of 33.83% from 2025 to 2033. The expansion of the AI market is driven by factors such as advancements in technology and increasing demand across various end-user sectors. The comprehensive report on the "Artificial Intelligence Market Global Report" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, providing insights on solutions, technologies, end-users, countries, and company analysis within the AI sector.
news.headline: AI market predicted to reach $2.53 trillion by 2033, growing at 33.83% CAGR from 2025. Major growth expected in global AI industry sectors.

news.summary: Pre-market on Tuesday, Marvell Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: MRVL) saw an increase in its shares. The company recently announced a definitive agreement to offload its Automotive Ethernet division to Infineon Technologies AG (OTC: IFNNY) for $2.5 billion in an all-cash transaction. This deal encompasses Marvell's Brightlane Automotive Ethernet portfolio and associated assets. Marvell foresees this sector to bring in revenue between $225 million to $250 million by 2025. For more details, refer to Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Marvell sells Auto Ethernet segment to Infineon Tech for $2.5B cash, expected to generate $225-250M revenue in 2025.

news.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer expressed concerns about the recent oversold conditions in the market following a tariff-induced selloff. The major indices had dipped into oversold territory over the last three sessions, prompting Cramer to question the Tuesday futures up move. Despite the technical readiness for a bounce, Cramer warned that the lack of clear positive news driving the futures surge could make investors wary of buying into the strength. He raised questions about the potential impact of China's retaliatory tariffs on the economy and whether the current buying activity was rational or a potential trap. Cramer advised caution due to the uncertain market conditions despite the positive momentum. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Jim Cramer warns against buying into oversold market rebound with unclear catalyst; questions if surge is a trap.

news.summary: Mitutoyo America Corporation, a top player in precision measurement tech, has unveiled the MiSTAR 575 Shop Floor CNC Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) in Aurora, Illinois. This advanced model builds on the triumph of the MiSTAR 555, pushing shop-floor metrics to greater levels. The MiSTAR 575 sets new standards in precision, performance, and measuring range, empowering diverse applications. Tailored for small to medium workpieces, this CMM offers unmatched capabilities with enhanced accuracy.
news.headline: Mitutoyo America unveils MiSTAR 575 Shop Floor CNC CMM for larger parts, offering next-level accuracy.

news.summary: Riboli Family Wines, a family business established in 1917, is excited to reveal a new look for its acclaimed Highlands 41 collection. The redesign includes updated packaging and website to reflect the brand's adventurous spirit. The wines, recognized with over 90 points and numerous awards, are based in Paso Robles, California. The changes aim to enhance the brand's identity and appeal to consumers seeking discovery and quality. The family is proud to introduce this transformation as they continue their tradition of excellence in winemaking.
news.headline: Riboli Family Wines redesigns Highlands 41 brand, with refreshed packaging and website. Family-run since 1917.

Brian Booth, the President of the company, identified a growing demand for a holistic "in-home solution" for family caregivers. The program aims to serve a significant number of seniors in Baldwin County by providing specialized care and support tailored to individual needs, catering to an estimated X number of families. The initiative seeks to improve the quality of life for seniors while alleviating the responsibilities of family members, reflecting a growing trend in senior care services across the nation.
news.headline: liveWELL Senior Living Services revolutionizing senior care for Baldwin County seniors in Alabama. President Brian Booth introduces 'in-home solution'.

news.summary: In Wall, New Jersey, New Jersey Resources (NYSE: NJR) is welcoming investors, customers, and financial community members to tune in to a live webcast unveiling its fiscal 2025 second-quarter financial results on May 6, 2025, at 10 a.m. Eastern Time. The results are set to be disclosed on May 5, 2025, after the stock market's closing time. To access the webcast, interested parties can visit www.njresources.com and navigate to the "Investor Relations" section a few minutes prior to the scheduled time.

news.summary: A Bitcoin user made a panic-induced error by paying a massive fee of nearly 0.75 BTC ($70,500) in a replace-by-fee (RBF) transaction. The transaction, attempted after midnight UTC on April 8, involved sending 0.48 Bitcoin ($37,770) with a change of 0.2 BTC ($16,357). Despite two RBF attempts, both transactions remain unconfirmed due to a subsequent higher-fee RBF transaction taking precedence. The error could have occurred due to confusion over fee settings or a bug in the automation script. RBF, a controversial Bitcoin feature, allows users to edit unconfirmed transactions by submitting new ones with higher fees, leading to potential double-spending issues. Bitcoin Cash has removed this feature to enhance security, but RBF-like transactions still occur.
news.headline: Bitcoin user mistakenly pays $70.5K in fees with 2 replace-by-fee transactions, totaling to 0.75 BTC ($70.5K) & 0.48 BTC ($37.77K) with 0.2 BTC of change ($16.36K). The incident occurred on April 8. RBF allows users to edit unconfirmed transactions by submitting an alternative transaction with a higher fee. Controversial feature due to potential double-spending. Bitcoin Cash has removed the feature, but similar transactions have occurred.

news.summary: Laser Photonics Corporation (NASDAQ: LASE) and subsidiary Control Micro Systems, Inc. have revealed a notable ramp-up in their collaborative Research and Development project concentrating on Laser Cold Marking technology for plastic medical device manufacturing. This initiative targets the precision needs of this industry.

news.summary: A former Chief Marketing Officer at Samsung, Marc Mathieu, who was a leader in artificial intelligence at Salesforce, has recently taken on a new role at Venn in New York. Venn aims to revolutionize the multifamily real estate sector using advanced technology. Mathieu will play a key role in developing and implementing artificial intelligence strategies to enhance Venn's operations. This move signifies a significant development in the industry, with Mathieu bringing his expertise to a company that is focused on integrating intelligent tech into the real estate market.

news.summary: In New York, Pangram, a leading AI text detection tool, has introduced a new model that boasts heightened accuracy levels, surpassing even the latest models such as those from OpenAI. This cutting-edge detector of AI-generated text is recognized for its precision and reliability in the field.
news.headline: Pangram releases new AI writing detection model, the most accurate on new models like OpenAI's.

news.summary: A technology company based in Menlo Park, California has introduced the DeltaStream Fusion Unified Analytics Platform, which allows organizations to efficiently handle both real-time and historical data within a single platform. This innovation aims to streamline data processing for businesses, offering a comprehensive solution for data analysis.
news.headline: DeltaStream unveils Fusion, the first unified analytics platform for real-time and historical data processing - consolidating data operations for organizations.

news.summary: Mathias Höglund, Kalmar’s SVP of Human Resources and member of the leadership team, is leaving the company as of April 8th, 2025. The company commends his role in strengthening HR processes, especially during the transition to an independent company. Kalmar is set to begin the recruitment process for a new SVP of Human Resources immediately. Internal arrangements are in place to ensure a smooth interim period. Kalmar operates globally in over 120 countries and has around 5,200 employees. In 2024, the company's sales reached approximately EUR 1.7 billion. For more information, contact Carina Geber-Teir at Kalmar.
news.headline: Kalmar's SVP, HR, Mathias Höglund leaving company. New recruitment process for replacement initiated. Company operates in 120+ countries, with 5,200 employees and sales of EUR 1.7 billion in 2024.

news.summary: In South Korea, Magnachip Semiconductor Corporation (NYSE: MX) has revealed its decision to close down its Display business by the end of Q2 2025 after a comprehensive review. The Board of Directors has unanimously approved this plan following the Company's previous announcement to evaluate all strategic possibilities for the Display business. The Display business will be categorized as discontinued operations starting from the release of Q1 results.
news.headline: Magnachip to close Display business by end of Q2 2025. Board unanimously approves shutdown plan.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Envoy, a workplace platform, has introduced a revamped analytics experience called Actionable Analytics. This launch comprises newly designed dashboards featuring interactive data visualizations centered on metrics like occupancy, attendance, desks, rooms, visitors, and workplace deliveries. Actionable Analytics provides companies with intuitive filters and custom reporting options to enable them to make data-driven decisions.
news.headline: Envoy unveils Actionable Analytics feature to track occupancy, attendance, desks, rooms, and visitors for informed decision-making.

The collaboration aims to boost retailer integration, diversify payment methods, and enhance security for merchants who accept the NationsBenefits Benefits Mastercard® Prepaid Flex Card. This prepaid card offers members convenient access to various benefits.

news.summary: Jonathan Rende, a seasoned technology professional, has been named as the new Chief Product Officer (CPO) at Checkmarx in Paramus, N.J. Rende will assume the role immediately at the forefront of the cloud-native application security leader.

news.summary: In Houston, SynergenX is raising awareness during Stress Awareness Month in April about the detrimental effects of chronic stress on health, particularly hormone balance. The company highlights the link between prolonged stress and hormone imbalance, emphasizing the significance of recognizing this connection. To aid individuals in assessing if stress is impacting their hormonal health, SynergenX provides a convenient self-evaluation tool. Excessive stress leads to the overproduction of hormones, indicating the crucial need to address stress levels for overall well-being.
news.headline: Chronic stress can lead to hormone imbalance, impacting health. SynergenX offers self-assessment tool during Stress Awareness Month.

news.summary: A digital health company based in Peachtree Corners, Georgia, known as Sanguina, has unveiled Ruby, marking a significant rebrand and extension of its AnemoCheck Mobile app. The updated app incorporates the company's patented fingernail selfie method for tracking blood health and introduces additional functionalities such as circulation assessment, wellness monitoring, and customized health tips. Ruby goes beyond the app's initial emphasis on managing anemia. It integrates the same patented technology for enhanced user experience.
news.headline: Sanguina launches Ruby, an evolution of AnemoCheck Mobile, with features like circulation analysis and wellness tracking.

news.summary: In Bentonville, Arkansas, Walmart+ is marking its success as a top membership in retail by hosting Walmart+ Week from April 28 to May 4. The exclusive event, dedicated to both current and new members, offers special rewards and deals to enhance the existing benefits. Members will enjoy perks such as 50 cents off per gallon of fuel at select Exxon™ and Mobil™ stations nationwide during this week-long celebration.
news.headline: Walmart+ Week offers exclusive savings for members from April 28 to May 4, including 50¢ off per gallon of fuel.

news.summary: In Deerfield, Illinois, Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. disclosed its financial outcomes for the second quarter of fiscal 2025, concluding on February 28, 2025. The CEO, Tim Wentworth, highlighted that the results for the quarter demonstrated effective cost control and enhancements in U.S. Healthcare. However, this progress was somewhat counterbalanced by poorer front-end outcomes in U.S. Retail Pharmacy. The company also faced significant legal settlements that led to persistent negative free cash flow.
news.headline: Walgreens Boots Alliance reports Q2 fiscal 2025 results: CEO cites cost management, improvement in US Healthcare, and negative free cash flow due to legal settlements.

news.summary: In San Diego, WD-40 Company (NASDAQ:WDFC) announced their financial results for the second fiscal quarter ending on February 28, 2025. Total net sales for the quarter reached $146.1 million, marking a 5% growth from the previous year's fiscal quarter. This indicates a steady increase in the company's sales performance.
news.headline: WD-40 Company's Q2 2025: Net sales $146.1 million, up 5% from prior year. Creating solutions in workshops, factories, homes.

news.summary: Salt Lake City — Recently, 50 grocery suppliers are preparing to join ReposiTrak for its cutting-edge traceability solutions, all before regulatory deadlines. This move comes as ReposiTrak offers hardware-free solutions to facilitate compliance with regulations within the industry.
